channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Iran opens Strait of Hormuz to some ships amid escalating strikes on Gulf countries
item.description: Iran opens select ships through the Strait of Hormuz, but most oil and gas tankers are stalled as regional attacks escalate, with Gulf countries facing daily missile and drone strikes.
item.content:encoded: Iran opens select ships through the Strait of Hormuz, but most oil and gas tankers are stalled as regional attacks escalate, with Gulf countries facing daily missile and drone strikes.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: Former national security adviser John Bolton talks about the goals of the Iran war
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks John Bolton, President Trump's former national security adviser and a long-time advocate of regime change in Iran, whether that goal is being met in the war.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel asks John Bolton, President Trump's former national security adviser and a long-time advocate of regime change in Iran, whether that goal is being met in the war.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Iran war delays delivery of medical goods to other parts of the world
item.description: The war in Iran has slowed down international shipping, much of which contains medical and humanitarian goods destined for Asia and Africa.
item.content:encoded: The war in Iran has slowed down international shipping, much of which contains medical and humanitarian goods destined for Asia and Africa.
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis

item.title: Babies are an afterthought in the birthright citizenship case, advocates say
item.description: An end to birthright citizenship would mean a new layer of bureaucracy for all babies born in the U.S. and could cause delays for health insurance and other benefits.
item.content:encoded:
An end to birthright citizenship would mean a new layer of bureaucracy for all babies born in the U.S. and could cause delays for health insurance and other benefits.

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Iran opens Strait of Hormuz to some ships as Gulf countries face strikes, Trump's mixed messages on the war in Iran could hurt the GOP, TSA officers are receiving back pay as DHS remains unfunded.
item.content:encoded: Iran opens Strait of Hormuz to some ships as Gulf countries face strikes, Trump's mixed messages on the war in Iran could hurt the GOP, TSA officers are receiving back pay as DHS remains unfunded.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Creator of 'Scrubs' and 'Shrinking' talks about his new HBO series 'Rooster'
item.description: Television producer Bill Lawrence has a new show on HBO called "Rooster." It's the latest in a run of hits that's quietly built him a small comedy empire.
item.content:encoded: Television producer Bill Lawrence has a new show on HBO called "Rooster." It's the latest in a run of hits that's quietly built him a small comedy empire.
item.dc:creator: Eric Deggans

item.title: 'Life needs to be more like this': Some bars and restaurants are going phone free
item.description: It's not just schools that are banning cellphones. Some bars and restaurants are joining the offline trend.
item.content:encoded: It's not just schools that are banning cellphones. Some bars and restaurants are joining the offline trend.
item.dc:creator: Nick de la Canal

item.title: Rep. Jim Himes, D-Conn., discusses the White House's messaging on the war in Iran
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Rep. Jim Himes, a Connecticut Democrat and ranking member of the House Intelligence Committee, about the White House's messaging on the Iran war.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Rep. Jim Himes, a Connecticut Democrat and ranking member of the House Intelligence Committee, about the White House's messaging on the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Republican Party may face consequences for Trump's mixed messaging on the Iran war
item.description: President Trump's mixed messages make it hard to see how the war with Iran will end, and it may lead to political consequences for his party in the upcoming midterm elections.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's mixed messages make it hard to see how the war with Iran will end, and it may lead to political consequences for his party in the upcoming midterm elections.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram

item.title: California teachers amend lesson plans after Cesar Chavez abuse allegations
item.description: Public school teachers across California have taught Cesar Chavez's contributions to the labor movement for a long time. Now they're figuring out how to revise those lessons given the recent allegations against him of sexual assault.
item.content:encoded: Public school teachers across California have taught Cesar Chavez's contributions to the labor movement for a long time. Now they're figuring out how to revise those lessons given the recent allegations against him of sexual assault.
item.dc:creator: Katie DeBenedetti
